market_context.title: 英伟达(NVDA.O)(最新年结日01-25)的资本性支出、存货周转率(次)等
points: 归属于母公司股东的净利润: 1201亿美元

data_gaps: Cash and cash equivalents at period end are missing

data.message: Evidence review complete; emitting analysis cards.

data.title: Revenue Performance and Growth Trend
data.conclusion: NVIDIA achieved a record annual revenue of $215.94 billion in FY2026, driven by explosive demand in the Data Center segment.
data.evidence: According to the Income Statement, Revenue reached $215,938 million for the full year. The Trailing 12-quarter history shows a consistent upward trajectory, with quarterly revenue rising from $7.19 billion in 2024Q1 to $68.13 billion in 2026Q4.
data.cross_checks: The sum of the four quarters in FY2026 ($44.06B, $46.74B, $57.01B, and $68.13B) aligns with the reported annual total of $215.94 billion.
data.counterpoints: While annual growth is massive, the quarter-over-quarter growth rate in 2026Q4 slowed slightly compared to the triple-digit surges seen in early 2025.
data.investor_implication: The company has successfully scaled its top line to a new magnitude, but investors should monitor if the quarterly growth rate begins to plateau as the base becomes larger.

data.title: Profitability and Margin Analysis
data.conclusion: Profitability remains exceptionally high with Net Profit reaching $120.07 billion and Gross Margins stabilizing above 70%.
data.evidence: Net profit attributable to shareholders for FY2026 was $120,067 million. The Trailing 12-quarter history indicates that Sales Gross Margin remained robust, ending the year at 71.07% in 2026Q4.
data.cross_checks: The Sales Net Margin is reported at 55.60% in the Key Ratios, confirming that over half of every dollar earned is converted into bottom-line profit.
data.counterpoints: Gross margin peaked at 78.35% in 2025Q1 and has since trended slightly lower to 71.07% by 2026Q4, suggesting a shift in product mix or increased supply chain costs.
data.investor_implication: NVIDIA maintains best-in-class profitability, though the slight compression in gross margins from the 2025 peak warrants attention regarding long-term pricing power.

data.title: Business Segment Composition
data.conclusion: The Data Center business has become the absolute pillar of NVIDIA, accounting for nearly 90% of total revenue.
data.evidence: The Main Business Composition table shows Data Center products generated $193.74 billion, representing 89.72% of total revenue. Gaming GPUs followed at $16.04 billion (7.43%).
data.cross_checks: Total revenue across all segments (Data Center, Gaming, Professional Visualization, Auto, and Others) sums to the consolidated $215.94 billion.
data.counterpoints: The extreme concentration in Data Center makes the company highly sensitive to capital expenditure cycles of a few major cloud service providers.
data.investor_implication: NVIDIA is now primarily an AI infrastructure company rather than a diversified chipmaker; its valuation is tied almost entirely to the AI investment cycle.

data.title: Cash Flow and Capital Allocation
data.conclusion: Strong operations generated over $100 billion in cash, supporting aggressive share repurchases and continued R&D.
data.evidence: Operating cash flow for FY2026 was $102,718 million. The Cash flow statement shows $40.09 billion spent on 'Reduction of share capital' (buybacks) and $18.50 billion invested in R&D.
data.cross_checks: Net cash increase for the year was $2.02 billion, as massive operating inflows were largely offset by investing ($52.23B) and financing ($48.47B) outflows.
data.counterpoints: Capital expenditures reached $6.04 billion, which is relatively low compared to revenue, reflecting NVIDIA's efficient fabless manufacturing model.
data.investor_implication: The company's ability to generate cash is unparalleled, allowing it to return significant value to shareholders while outspending competitors on innovation.

data.title: Balance Sheet Strength and Liquidity
data.conclusion: NVIDIA maintains a fortress balance sheet with low leverage and high asset turnover.
data.evidence: Total Assets stood at $206.80 billion against Total Liabilities of $49.51 billion. The Debt-to-asset ratio improved to 23.94% by the end of FY2026.
data.cross_checks: Current assets of $125.61 billion significantly exceed current liabilities of $32.16 billion, indicating excellent short-term liquidity.
data.counterpoints: Inventory levels reached $21.40 billion. While the Inventory turnover ratio is healthy at 3.97x, the absolute dollar value of inventory has grown alongside the business scale.
data.investor_implication: The low debt levels and massive cash reserves provide NVIDIA with significant strategic flexibility for future acquisitions or economic downturns.

data.title: Operational Efficiency and Returns
data.conclusion: The company is delivering extraordinary returns on equity, reflecting its dominant market position in AI.
data.evidence: Key Ratios show a Net Asset Reporting (ROE) of 101.49% and a Total Asset Net Margin of 75.42% for FY2026.
data.cross_checks: The high ROE is driven by the high Net Profit Margin (55.60%) and efficient asset utilization, rather than excessive financial leverage.
data.counterpoints: Maintaining a 100%+ ROE is historically difficult as competition intensifies and the equity base grows through retained earnings.
data.investor_implication: NVIDIA's current operational efficiency is at a level rarely seen in the semiconductor industry, justifying its premium market valuation.

data.title: Trailing 12-Quarter Trend Analysis
data.conclusion: The 3-year trend shows a fundamental transformation from a $7B/quarter company to a $68B/quarter powerhouse.
data.evidence: The Trailing 12-quarter history shows Revenue growing from $7.19B (2024Q1) to $68.13B (2026Q4). Net Profit grew even faster, from $2.04B to $42.96B over the same period.
data.cross_checks: The Debt-to-asset ratio has steadily declined from 44.85% in 2024Q1 to 23.94% in 2026Q4, showing that growth was financed through earnings rather than debt.
data.counterpoints: The most rapid phase of margin expansion occurred between 2024 and 2025; the 2026 period shows more stabilized, albeit high, margin levels.
data.investor_implication: The historical data confirms that NVIDIA has successfully navigated the 'hyper-growth' phase of the AI era while simultaneously strengthening its financial position.

data.step: Review passed for Revenue Performance and Growth Trend

data.rationale: The annual revenue of $215.94 billion and the quarterly trend from $7.19 billion to $68.13 billion are accurately derived from the income statement and trailing 12-quarter history.

data.step: Review passed for Profitability and Margin Analysis

data.rationale: Net profit of $120.07 billion and the 71.07% gross margin align with the current-period core metrics and source data tables.

data.step: Review passed for Business Segment Composition

data.rationale: The segment breakdown, specifically the 89.72% revenue contribution from Data Center products, is verified by the main business composition data.

data.step: Review passed for Cash Flow and Capital Allocation

data.rationale: Operating cash flow of $102.72 billion and the capital allocation details (buybacks and R&D) are consistent with the cash flow statement.

data.step: Review passed for Balance Sheet Strength and Liquidity

data.rationale: Total assets, liabilities, and the debt-to-asset ratio of 23.94% match the period-end balance sheet figures.

data.summary: NVIDIA has completed a historic transformation into the primary infrastructure provider for the AI era, characterized by record-breaking revenue and elite profitability levels. NVIDIA enters FY2027 as a dominant market leader with unparalleled financial strength, though its valuation remains closely tied to the sustainability of the global AI investment cycle.

NVIDIA has completed a historic transformation into the primary infrastructure provider for the AI era, characterized by record-breaking revenue and elite profitability levels. NVIDIA enters FY2027 as a dominant market leader with unparalleled financial strength, though its valuation remains closely tied to the sustainability of the global AI investment cycle.

resolved_points.rationale: The annual revenue of $215.94 billion and the quarterly trend from $7.19 billion to $68.13 billion are accurately derived from the income statement and trailing 12-quarter history.
Net profit of $120.07 billion and the 71.07% gross margin align with the current-period core metrics and source data tables.
The segment breakdown, specifically the 89.72% revenue contribution from Data Center products, is verified by the main business composition data.
Operating cash flow of $102.72 billion and the capital allocation details (buybacks and R&D) are consistent with the cash flow statement.
Total assets, liabilities, and the debt-to-asset ratio of 23.94% match the period-end balance sheet figures.

source_refs.field: NVIDIA's inventory levels reached $21.40 billion by the end of FY2026, with an inventory turnover ratio of 3.97x.
